prompt.content:
You are a spatial reasoning assistant analyzing a 3D scene image.
You will receive a list of objects visible in the image and a set of distance comparison questions.

For each question, compare 3D distances — either between two pairs of objects,
or from a common anchor object to two candidate objects.
Answer with exactly one of: "<" (first pair/candidate closer), "~=" (approximately equal), ">" (first pair/candidate farther).

Respond ONLY with a JSON array. Each element must have "qid" and "answer" (a string: "<", "~=", or ">").

Example:
[{"qid": "qrr_0001", "answer": "<"}, {"qid": "qrr_0002", "answer": "~="}]

content.text:
Objects in the image:
  - obj_0: small gray metal cylinder
  - obj_1: small blue metal cylinder
  - obj_2: large cyan rubber sphere
  - obj_3: large cyan rubber cylinder
  - obj_4: small yellow metal cube

Questions:
[qrr_0001] Compare the distance between obj_0 and obj_1 vs the distance between obj_2 and obj_3. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0002] Compare the distance between obj_0 and obj_1 vs the distance between obj_2 and obj_4. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0003] Compare the distance between obj_0 and obj_1 vs the distance between obj_3 and obj_4. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0004] Compare the distance between obj_0 and obj_2 vs the distance between obj_1 and obj_3. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0005] Compare the distance between obj_0 and obj_2 vs the distance between obj_1 and obj_4. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0006] Compare the distance between obj_0 and obj_2 vs the distance between obj_3 and obj_4. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0007] Compare the distance between obj_0 and obj_3 vs the distance between obj_1 and obj_2. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0008] Compare the distance between obj_0 and obj_3 vs the distance between obj_1 and obj_4. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0009] Compare the distance between obj_0 and obj_3 vs the distance between obj_2 and obj_4. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0010] Compare the distance between obj_0 and obj_4 vs the distance between obj_1 and obj_2. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0011] Compare the distance between obj_0 and obj_4 vs the distance between obj_1 and obj_3. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0012] Compare the distance between obj_0 and obj_4 vs the distance between obj_2 and obj_3. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0013] Compare the distance between obj_1 and obj_2 vs the distance between obj_3 and obj_4. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0014] Compare the distance between obj_1 and obj_3 vs the distance between obj_2 and obj_4. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0015] Compare the distance between obj_1 and obj_4 vs the distance between obj_2 and obj_3. Answer: < / ~= / >

raw_response:
I need to analyze the 3D positions of objects across multiple images showing the same scene from different angles.

Let me identify object positions from the images:

Looking at the images carefully:
- obj_0: small gray metal cylinder (center area)
- obj_1: small blue metal cylinder (right side or behind)
- obj_2: large cyan rubber sphere (behind or right area)
- obj_3: large cyan rubber cylinder (front/left area)
- obj_4: small yellow metal cube (center area)

From the multiple views, I can estimate approximate 3D positions:
- obj_3 (cyan cylinder): front-left area
- obj_4 (yellow cube): center
- obj_0 (gray cylinder): center, slightly right
- obj_1 (blue cylinder): right side or behind
- obj_2 (cyan sphere): back area

Estimating distances:
- obj_0 to obj_1: ~small-medium distance
- obj_0 to obj_2: ~medium distance
- obj_0 to obj_3: ~medium-large distance
- obj_0 to obj_4: ~small distance
- obj_1 to obj_2: ~medium distance
- obj_1 to obj_3: ~large distance
- obj_1 to obj_4: ~medium distance
- obj_2 to obj_3: ~large distance
- obj_2 to obj_4: ~medium distance
- obj_3 to obj_4: ~medium-large distance
